hero.title: 7 Days in Sikkim.
hero.titleAccent: Kanchenjunga Views, Monasteries and Mountain Lakes.
hero.intro: Sikkim is the Himalayas at their greenest and most serene. Gangtok's monasteries, views of Kanchenjunga, the world's third-highest peak, the sacred Tsomgo Lake and the high Nathula pass. Seven days in the gentle eastern Himalayas.

overview.heading: 7 Days Sikkim & the Eastern Himalayas: The Full Picture
body: Sikkim, tucked into the eastern Himalayas between Nepal, Tibet and Bhutan, is one of India's most beautiful and peaceful states. Lush, green and dotted with Buddhist monasteries, it offers stunning views of Kanchenjunga, the world's third-highest mountain, alongside high mountain lakes, flower valleys and a gentle, welcoming culture.

This 7 day Sikkim package from Malaysia is arranged privately, reached via the gateway of Bagdogra near Darjeeling. Your own vehicle and driver guide take you to Gangtok and its monasteries, the sacred Tsomgo Lake, the high Nathula area, and viewpoints for Kanchenjunga, with permits arranged for the restricted high-altitude areas.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Kanchenjunga Views
items.body: The world's third-highest peak dominates the Sikkim skyline. Sunrise viewpoints and clear-weather vistas of Kanchenjunga are among the great mountain sights of India.

items.title: Gangtok & Monasteries
items.body: The hillside capital of Gangtok and the great monasteries like Rumtek, the seat of Tibetan Buddhism's Karmapa, full of prayer wheels and murals.

items.title: Tsomgo Lake & Nathula
items.body: The sacred high-altitude Tsomgo Lake and the road towards the Nathula pass on the old Silk Route to Tibet, dramatic high-mountain landscapes.

days.title: Arrive Bagdogra, to Gangtok
days.description: Met at Bagdogra airport and driven up to Gangtok, the Sikkim capital.

days.title: Gangtok Monasteries
days.description: The monasteries and sights around Gangtok.

days.title: Gangtok to Pelling
days.description: Drive west towards Pelling for Kanchenjunga views.

days.title: Pelling & Kanchenjunga
days.description: Sunrise views and waterfalls around Pelling.

days.title: Pelling to Darjeeling area
days.description: Drive towards the Darjeeling area or back towards Bagdogra with tea-country views.

days.title: Depart Bagdogra
days.description: Transfer to Bagdogra airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: The Eastern Himalayas, Expertly Arranged
whyBook.body: Sikkim's restricted areas need permits, and the mountain roads reward local knowledge. We handle the permits, the routing and the timing for the best Kanchenjunga views. Dhesu has arranged Himalayan journeys for many years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Sikkim Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
Sikkim is one of India's most beautiful and peaceful states, set in the lush eastern Himalayas. This 7 day package, reached via Bagdogra, covers Gangtok, the monasteries, the sacred Tsomgo Lake and the Kanchenjunga views around Pelling.

A private vehicle and driver guide handle the mountain roads, and we arrange the permits required for the high-altitude restricted areas.

It is ideal for travellers seeking gentle Himalayan scenery, Buddhist culture and one of the great mountain panoramas in India.

sections.heading: Kanchenjunga and Sikkim's Monasteries
sections.body:
Kanchenjunga, the world's third-highest mountain at over 8,500 metres, is the guardian peak of Sikkim, revered by locals and visible from many viewpoints in clear weather.

Sikkim is also rich in Buddhist heritage, with monasteries like Rumtek and Pemayangtse offering serene atmosphere, prayer wheels and intricate murals. Together they give the state its peaceful, spiritual character.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Bagdogra Airport (IXB). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Sikkim?
items.answer: March to May offers blooming flowers and clear views, while October to November brings crisp air and the best Kanchenjunga vistas. The monsoon (June to September) is lush but wet, with possible landslides. Winter is cold with snow at higher points.

items.question: Do I need permits for Sikkim?
items.answer: Yes. Foreign visitors need an Inner Line Permit for Sikkim, and additional permits for restricted areas like Tsomgo Lake and Nathula. We assist with arranging these as part of the package, and will request the necessary documents in advance.

items.question: Will I definitely see Kanchenjunga?
items.answer: Views depend on the weather, which is clearest in spring and autumn. We plan viewpoints and timing to maximise your chances, but mountain weather can never be guaranteed. The monasteries, lakes and scenery make the trip rewarding regardless.
